2604.21816.title: Tool Attention Is All You Need: Dynamic Tool Gating and Lazy Schema Loading for Eliminating the MCP/Tools Tax in Scalable Agentic Workflows
2604.21816.paper_summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has become a common interface for connecting large language model (LLM) agents to external tools, but its reliance on stateless, eager schema injection imposes a hidden per-turn overhead the MCP Tax or Tools Tax that practitioner reports place between roughly 10k and 60k tokens in typical multi-server deployments. This payload inflates the key-value cache, is associated with reasoning degradation as context utilization approaches published fracture points around 70%, and turns token budgets into a recurring operational cost. We introduce Tool Attention, a middleware-layer mechanism that generalizes the "Attention Is All You Need" paradigm from self-attention over tokens to gated attention over tools. Tool Attention combines (i) an Intent Schema Overlap (ISO) score from sentence embeddings, (ii) a state-aware gating function enforcing preconditions and access scopes, and (iii) a two-phase lazy schema loader that keeps a compact summary pool in context and promotes full JSON schemas only for top-k gated tools. We evaluate on a simulated 120-tool, six-server benchmark whose per-server token counts are calibrated to public audits of real MCP deployments. In this simulation, Tool Attention directly reduces measured per-turn tool tokens by 95.0% (47.3k -> 2.4k) and raises effective context utilization (a token-ratio quantity) from 24% to 91%. End-to-end figures for task success, latency, cost, and reasoning quality are reported as projections derived from the measured token counts combined with published deployment telemetry; they are not measured on live LLM agents, and we mark projected values explicitly throughout. Taken together, the results support a simple thesis: protocol-level efficiency, not raw context length, is a binding constraint on scalable gentic systems. The code for this work is accessible at https://github.com/asadani/tool-attention
2604.21816.paper_summary_zh: 模型上下文協議（MCP）已成為將大型語言模型（LLM）代理連接到外部工具的常見介面，但它對無狀態、急切的架構注入的依賴，對每次交互施加了隱藏的每回合開銷，即 MCP 稅或工具稅，從實踐者報告中可見，這在典型的多伺服器部署中約為 10,000 到 60,000 個標記。這一負載使得鍵值快取膨脹，並與推理退化相關，當上下文利用率接近 70% 的已發表斷裂點時，並將標記預算轉變為一種持續的運營成本。我們介紹了工具注意力（Tool Attention），這是一種中介層機制，將「注意力就是你所需要的一切」的範式從對標記的自注意力推廣到對工具的門控注意力。工具注意力結合了（i）來自句子嵌入的意圖架構重疊（ISO）分數，（ii）一個狀態感知的門控函數，用於強制預條件和訪問範圍，以及（iii）一個兩階段的懶惰架構加載器，保持上下文中的緊湊摘要池，並僅為前 k 個門控工具推廣完整的 JSON 架構。我們在一個模擬的 120 工具、六伺服器基準上進行評估，其每台伺服器的標記數量經過調整，以符合對實際 MCP 部署的公共審計。在這個模擬中，工具注意力直接將每回合測量的工具標記減少了 95.0%（47.3k -> 2.4k），並將有效的上下文利用率（標記比率量）從 24% 提高到 91%。任務成功率、延遲、成本和推理質量的端到端數據被報告為從測量的標記數量結合已發表的部署遙測推導出的預測值；這些數據並未在實時 LLM 代理上進行測量，我們在整個過程中明確標記預測值。綜合來看，結果支持一個簡單的論點：協議層級的效率，而非原始上下文長度，是可擴展基因系統的約束條件。這項工作的代碼可在 https://github.com/asadani/tool-attention 獲取。
2604.21816.author: Anuj Sadani et.al.
